column1: the three factors studied (concentration, time, and speed of homogenization) aﬀect the eﬃciency of

encapsulation and modify the release proﬁle of mangiferin.
Interactions between the molecules in the
formulation aﬀected the ﬁngerprint of the compounds when encapsulated; however, this formation of
bonds does not produce a negative eﬀect on the antipopoisomerase activity of mangiferin and does not
present hepatotoxicity in vitro.
